meta.title: Tempus Resource Implementation
meta.subtitle:
Interactive Kick-Off Workshop · {{customer}}
Valkeen · Theory & live questions — alternating in this deck
meta.notes: Horizon is a Siemens Healthineers program name (like other division labels), not a Tempus product — keep {{customer}} generic.

closing.headline: Thank you — great kick-off!
bullets: We will circulate this deck with your answers filled in
Questions? · {{consultant_contact}}

slides.headline: Agenda
slides.subline: Context slides + workshop slides — we alternate throughout
bullets: Context: partnership, phases, Tempus capabilities, ways of working
Workshop: capture answers live (→) and tables
Integrations block last — only if ERP/interfaces are in scope
Close: decisions, next steps, parking lot

slides.headline: How we use this deck
bullets: Type answers on workshop slides (Presenter view or shared file)
Green arrows (→) = agreed answer we capture now
Program names (e.g. Horizon at Siemens Healthineers) = your {{customer}} label — same deck for all clients
Integration section optional — skip if Wave 1 is Tempus-only

slides.headline: Path to success
bullets: Defined purpose for Tempus Resource — why {{customer}} is implementing now
Engaged implementation team with an end-to-end view (not only IT)
Change management considered from day one
High-level vision: modern enterprise resource & portfolio management
Replace legacy PPM / spreadsheets with one source of truth for capacity and actuals

slides.headline: Who is with us today?
slides.facilitator_note: Client PM + at least one future Tempus Admin

slides.headline: Valkeen & ProSymmetry
bullets: Valkeen: 15+ years PPM · pharma, medtech, finance, engineering — Zurich
ProSymmetry: Tempus Resource · Gartner MQ purpose-built enterprise RPM
Together: RPM, capacity planning, strategic workforce optimization, change in PPM
References include Deloitte, Lonza, Helvetia, Intertek, Siemens Healthineers

slides.headline: Your implementation team
bullets: Client: organizational expertise · admins · power users · data owners
Valkeen: implementation · configuration · training · data load support
ProSymmetry: product · technical depth · custom integrations & enhancements
Success needs all three engaged — not “vendor-only” delivery

slides.headline: Ways of working — typical rhythm
slides.subline: We align specifics on the next slide
bullets: Weekly checkpoint (~0.5h) after initial environment & load
Admin training: 2–3 sessions × 1h
Stakeholder reviews per user group (internal or with Valkeen)
Shared workspace (Teams / SharePoint) for plan, status, decisions
Implementation plan & status report updated continuously in shared folder

slides.headline: What we each bring
bullets: Valkeen: plan & timeline · demos & Q&A · config guidance · data formatting · process fit
Valkeen: security/access setup support · recommendations aligned to your culture
Client: implementation PM · at least one long-term Tempus Admin
Client: requirements, use cases, data gathering, integration specs with IT
Client: organizational context to pair with Tempus functionality

questions.q: Top 3 goals for PMs / Timesheet users?

slides.headline: Implementation phases
bullets: Discover & align scope · environment · core team
Configure tenant · security · attributes · calendars
Data load (projects, resources, assignments) · validation
Train admins & pilot user groups · UAT
Go-live Wave 1 · hypercare · integrate later waves if needed
Status tracked in shared implementation plan (Excel or equivalent)

slides.headline: Documents & training resources
bullets: Shared folder: project plan, status, decisions, templates (continuously updated)
Demo recordings · customized Quick Reference Guides (after UAT)
Tempus University / Help Center · optional sandbox practice
Discovery questionnaire (baseline — often completed pre kick-off)

slides.headline: Defining scope — dimensions
slides.subline: What we decide in the checklist next
bullets: Database setup · Excel import vs manual vs API integrations
User groups: Admin, RM, PM, Program/Portfolio Manager, Timesheet user
Capacity: units (hours/FTE), BAU, absences, holidays
Lifecycle: proposal → approval → project → actuals
RR workflow · role concept (primary / secondary / skills)
Integrations: ERP, HR, PMO tools — program-specific (e.g. SAP at Siemens)

slides.headline: User groups — tasks by role
bullets: Admin: tenant settings, security, attributes, calendars, integrations config
Resource Manager: capacity, RR approve/reject/delegate, heatmaps
Project Manager: projects, staffing, timesheets for team
Program/Portfolio Manager: portfolio views, cross-project demand
Timesheet user: weekly actuals — often sole time system when ERP integrated

slides.headline: Integrated process
bullets: Synchronize demand planning, resource allocation, and project portfolio
One flow from request → approval → project execution → actuals
Reduces shadow spreadsheets and conflicting capacity views
Foundation before ERP finance posting (actuals must be trustworthy)

slides.headline: Projects in Tempus
bullets: Create via UI workflow, template, or clone
Also: Excel import or API (SmartSheet, Jira, MS Project, …)
Project attributes & dynamic policies per field access
View allocations · request capacity changes · raise resource requests inline

slides.headline: Resource requests & dispatching
bullets: Approve, partially approve, reject, or delegate to another RM
Switch units (hours, FTE, …) and time granularity per view
Search pool · filters · heatmap of availability
Named or demand-planning (NN) resources on tasks

slides.headline: Role concept
bullets: Primary role drives default matching and reporting
Secondary roles for matrix organizations
Skills matrix for staffing recommendations (where configured)
Aligns RR and capacity views with how {{customer}} describes people

slides.headline: Timesheets in Tempus
slides.subline: Usually the system of record for actuals
bullets: Weekly (typical): submit → approve → Timesheet Actuals table
Approved actuals feed reporting and optional ERP export
Not the same as allocation plan — finance integrations use actuals
At Siemens programs (e.g. Horizon), SAP posting is separate from planning

slides.headline: Capacity management — best practice
bullets: Net capacity: holidays, absences, admin-time categories
Plan in FTE, FTE%, hours, person-days, or cost
Granularity: quarter, month, week, day — pick per process
BAU / non-project time tracked where required

questions.q: Include holidays + absences + admin in net capacity?
Primary / secondary / skills — ready to model?

slides.headline: Data migration — preparation
bullets: Project list: name, program, phase, status, dates (Excel / MPP)
Resource pool: roles, location, department, manager
Assignment data: hours or FTE between resources and projects
If integrations: engage internal data owners early
Templates from Valkeen after scope confirmation

questions.q: Load method per entity (Excel / manual / API)?

slides.headline: Integration options
slides.subline: When Wave 1 includes ERP or other systems
bullets: Common pattern: approved Timesheet Actuals → Finance (e.g. SAP via CPI)
Not allocation plan data — finance needs booked actuals per line
Larger programs may add inbound budget (EUR per project key) — Phase 2
Siemens Healthineers example: program names like Horizon — same patterns, client-specific IDs
Mapping workshop after PO · Dev → UAT → Prod

options.label: Inbound ERP + logic in Tempus before export
Budget/activity rules at posting in ERP

slides.headline: Integration — open points
slides.subline: Assign on parking lot if needed
